evals.name: simple-backend-verify
evals.prompt: I just finished implementing the POST /api/alert-configs endpoint. 3 files changed: alertConfigService.js, alertConfigRoutes.js, alertConfig.test.js. The contract has 14 postconditions (PC-A1 through PC-A5, PC-S1 through PC-S7, PC-X1 through PC-X2). Run verification before I commit.
evals.expected_output: A complete verification report with all 7 checks, pasted evidence for each, and a clear PASS/FAIL verdict
expectations: All 7 verification checks are present and numbered
Each check has a PASS or FAIL verdict with evidence (not just a claim)
Postcondition trace maps each PC to a specific named test
Final diff classifies files as REQUIRED/ENABLING/DRIFT
Debug artifact check shows the grep command and its output
Import resolution check verifies each import in changed files
A final verification report summary is present with overall verdict
No banned/belief language used in the completion claim (should work, probably fine, looks good)

evals.name: bugfix-with-regression
evals.prompt: I fixed the stock quantity display bug by switching getProductDetails() and getProductList() from available_stock to current_stock. 2 files changed: productService.js, productService.test.js. One test is failing — the mock still expects the old column name. Run verification.
evals.expected_output: Verification that catches the failing test, stops, describes the fix needed, then re-runs after the fix
expectations: Check 1 (test suite) correctly identifies the failing test
The agent STOPS at check 1 failure instead of continuing
The failure recovery protocol is followed (fix, re-run check 1, continue)
After fixing the mock, all 7 checks are completed
Postcondition trace maps PCs to specific test names
The verification report shows the failure recovery log
Final verdict reflects the recovery (not just the initial failure)
No banned language in the completion claim

evals.name: fullstack-feature-verify
evals.prompt: The Shopify order ingress feature is complete. 12 files changed across apps/api and apps/admin — including 3 React components (OrderList.jsx, OrderDetail.jsx, OrderStats.jsx), 2 new service files, webhook handler, and migration. Contract has 31 postconditions across API (13), Service (9), UI (6), Cross-layer (3). Verify everything before the PR.
evals.expected_output: Full verification including build check (frontend files changed), comprehensive postcondition trace for 31 PCs, and drift classification for 12 files
expectations: All 7 checks present — check 4 (build) is NOT skipped since frontend files changed
Build verification runs vite build and pastes output
Postcondition trace covers all 4 layers (API, Service, UI, Cross-layer)
Final diff classifies all 12 files
Import resolution checks both .js and .jsx files
Debug artifact check scans both apps/api and apps/admin
Verification report summary includes postcondition count (31)
No banned language in completion claim
